text: I find myself surrounded by glass, steel, and the murmur of technological activity. There is a sense of order in this space: meetings, experiments, silent anticipation. The glass partitions create boundaries yet also invite glimpses of collaboration. I feel like I am both participant and observer—a bit detached, sensing potential in the room but also the impersonality of the modern world. The bright lighting and open lines of sight make me keenly aware of my presence here, yet I feel a gentle unease, as though I am expected to perform in a play whose script hovers just out of reach.
